reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of in-situ-generated nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N-O bond (partial, nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate via dehydration)

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents premixed in DES as a single feed. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization to furoxan. DES serves as both solvent and promoter. No incompatibility between phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate at room temperature.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to ensure full conversion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.equiv_or_loading: 1:5 molar ratio with EG (1 mL DES total)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent; acts as reaction medium and may promote nitrile oxide formation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (EtO2C-CNO) in situ. The DES medium likely facilitates this through hydrogen-bond-assisted dehydration.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively. The HOMO(dipole)-LUMO(dipolarophile) interaction favors the 3,5-disubstituted regioisomer.

stream_logic.reasoning: Single premixed feed. Both substrates are stable together at room temperature. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide is generated (upon heating) to trap it immediately and suppress dimerization to furoxan. DES is viscous (~50-100 cP at RT) so preheating the feed line or using a slightly elevated feed temperature (40-50°C) may aid pumpability. Concentration: phenylacetylene at 0.5 M, ethyl nitroacetate at 1.0 M in DES.

mixing_order_reasoning: All reagents are premixed in the DES at room temperature before entering the heated coil reactor. This ensures phenylacetylene is present to trap the nitrile oxide as soon as it forms at 120°C, minimizing furoxan (nitrile oxide dimer) side product. No separate mixing stage is needed since the substrates are mutually compatible at ambient temperature.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species involved. The reaction proceeds thermally in air.
quench_reasoning: No inline quench needed. The batch protocol quenches with water post-reaction for workup purposes only. In flow, the product stream can be collected and worked up offline (water addition, DCM extraction, drying).
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide cycloaddition). The DES viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT, lower at 120°C) is a practical concern for flow — may require wider-bore tubing (e.g., 1.0 mm ID) or preheating to reduce backpressure. The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate is used because nitrile oxide generation is not quantitative (some decomposition/dimerization). Residence time of ~15 min at 120°C should translate well to a heated coil reactor. Pressure rating should accommodate operation above the boiling point of any volatile components (phenylacetylene bp 143°C, so 120°C is below bp — manageable). Uncertain whether DES viscosity will cause significant pressure drop issues in narrow-bore tubing; recommend 0.75-1.0 mm ID PFA coil with back-pressure regulator (5-10 bar).
